production.playableLoop: Run through a three-lane metro track, switch lanes, jump barriers, duck low gates, collect coins, dodge trains, and clear score objectives before retrying after a crash.
production.objective: Collect 12 coins and run 260 meters while staying alive.
production.progression: Runner speed increases with distance, hazards recycle faster, and score rises from both distance and coins.
production.failRetry: Colliding with a train, failing to jump a red barrier, or failing to duck a low gate enters failed state; R or Enter resets the route.
feedbackMoments: Runner lane changes are visible during input-driven playtest proof.
Coins update HUD progress when collected.
Jump barriers, duck gates, and trains produce crash/retry state on failed avoidance.
Distance and score HUD text advance while the runner survives.

assetPlan.playerHero: Local custom GLB runner-thief model with runner silhouette and metro heist styling.
assetPlan.obstacleEnemy: Local custom GLB metro train, jump-barrier, and duck-gate hazards.
assetPlan.rewardInteractable: Local custom GLB coin collectibles placed across the three-lane route.
assetPlan.worldEnvironment: Local custom GLB metro rails, platforms, signs, skyline, speed lines, and set dressing.
assetPlan.uiHud: Retained HUD source for score, distance, coins, retry, objective, and mobile/control states.
assetPlan.audioFeedback: Goal-ping audio asset retained as feedback source; current gameplay proof is visual/HUD focused.

assetProvenance.fallback: The player, obstacles, rewards, metro rails, platforms, and signage are backed by a cohesive local custom GLB kit generated with @gltf-transform. Primitive geometry remains only as explicit runtime fallback because the required catalog database is unavailable.
